---
title: Slow tomato sauce
date: 2024-08-18
tags: [cooking, gardening, italian, tomatoes]
---

The sauce starts in the garden. I grow San Marzano and Principe Borghese side by side, one for richness and one for sweetness, and I pick them only when the shoulders have gone fully red. Underripe fruit makes thin, sour sauce no matter how long it simmers.

In the kitchen I blanch and peel the tomatoes, then crush them by hand into a wide pan with a generous glug of olive oil, a smashed garlic clove, and a whole basil sprig. Low heat and an hour of patience do more for the flavor than any ingredient list.

I freeze the finished sauce in flat bags that stack like books. Pulled out on a winter evening with a ladle of pasta water and a handful of grated cheese, it is the closest thing I have to a time machine.
